---
title: "Brief mindfulness guidance reduces implicit gender stereotypes during reading"
author: "Hung, P.-Y., Chen, Y.-Y., & Yeh, L.-H."
image: "../../assets/study-covers-en/2023-hung-gender-stereotype.png"
lang: en
language: ../../assets/language-en.yml
toc-title: "On this page"
---

```{r}
#| include: false
source("../../R/bmaa_plot_theme.R")
```

::: {.study-cover}
<img src="../../assets/study-covers-en/2023-hung-gender-stereotype.png" alt="Stereotype interference">

Cover figure: after training with BMAA-derived techniques, the rereading-time difference between incongruent and congruent passages decreased.
:::

## Method

A mixed-design eye-tracking study comparing a brief mindfulness guidance group with a waitlist control group. The practice included BMAA-derived warm-up exercises followed by breath observation and body scanning.

## Key Findings

This study used eye tracking to test whether readers reread more when gender stereotypes were violated. Longer rereading time suggests that the reader found the content unexpected and needed to reinterpret it. If incongruent passages trigger longer rereading, implicit gender stereotypes are influencing reading.

After brief mindfulness guidance, the mindfulness group no longer showed the same incongruency effect in third-paragraph rereading time, while the control group did not show the same pattern.

```{r}
#| fig-height: 4.8
dat <- data.frame(
  outcome = "Third-paragraph rereading time",
  group = rep(c("Mindfulness-congruent", "Mindfulness-incongruent", "Control-congruent", "Control-incongruent"), each = 2),
  time = rep(c("Pretest", "Posttest"), 4),
  value = c(1551.94, 2566.15, 3173.46, 1765.30, 2106.19, 1711.23, 2012.86, 1739.40)
)
bmaa_facet_line_plot(dat, "Brief mindfulness guidance reduced gender-incongruency effects", ylab = "Rereading time (ms)")
```

## Reference

洪珮瑩, 陳苡禕, & 葉理豪. (2023). 短期正念引導降低閱讀時的內隱性別刻板印象. *教育心理學報, 54*(3), 515-536. https://doi.org/10.6251/BEP.202303_54(3).0001
